blocks.blockLabel: Modern vehicles are no longer just mechanical machines—they are connected digital systems. In India, even mid-range cars now come with
items: Brands like Tata Motors, Hyundai Motor India, and Mahindra & Mahindra offer connected car features through apps.
This means your car is also a data-generating device, similar to a smartphone.
The car continuously sends location data to servers
This data is accessible through mobile apps or infotainment systems
allow owners to track vehicle location in real time

blocks.blockLabel: If login credentials are shared (or not updated after separation), another person can

items: A single account controls multiple features
Multiple users may be added (family/shared use)
Ex-partners or family members may still have access
OTP-based logins can be misused if SIM access exists
In India, SIM-based identity makes this particularly sensitive.
Commands are sent from the mobile app to the vehicle via internet
Show continued control over the victim

blocks.blockLabel: Even if you disconnect your phone
items: Data may remain in the system
Anyone using the car later can access it
Ride history can reveal personal routines
In stalking cases, this becomes a serious safety threat
Locking doors remotely while someone is inside
These actions create psychological pressure and fear
Cars registered in one person’s name
These are cheap and easily available in India
Alerts from car apps you didn’t trigger
Report to cyber cell if tracking is suspected
In India, cars are increasingly becoming digital surveillance tools if misused.
Awareness + control over access = main protection

blocks.blockLabel: In India, the adoption of smart devices has increased due to
items: Common brands used in India include Xiaomi, Amazon (Alexa devices), and Google (Google Nest ecosystem).
👉 These devices make homes convenient—but also introduce serious privacy and surveillance risks if misused.
Smart home devices are internet-connected systems that allow users to monitor, automate, and control household functions remotely.

subSections.subTitle: 1. Smart Security Cameras (CCTV / IP Cameras)

items: Unauthorized remote access (hacking or shared login)
Cameras placed in private spaces (illegal but still happens)

blocks.blockLabel: Cheap, unbranded CCTV cameras sold online often

items: Widely used in Indian homes, PGs, hostels, and apartments.
Common in urban apartments and Airbnb rentals
Often installed by landlords → tenants may not have full control
Linked accounts may expose personal data

blocks.blockLabel: If someone else controls the account, they may

items: Sudden switching ON/OFF to disturb or intimidate
Monitoring usage patterns (when lights are ON = when you are home)
Connect all devices into one system
If one account is compromised → entire home is accessible

subSections.subTitle: 1. Remote Surveillance
items: Cameras and microphones can be accessed remotely
Victims may be watched without their knowledge
In domestic conflict cases, this becomes covert monitoring

blocks.blockLabel: Mini spy cameras can be hidden in

items: Camera indicator lights turning ON unexpectedly
Cover Cameras When Not in Use
Avoid cheap, unknown devices with poor security
Report hidden cameras or surveillance to police/cyber cell
Review all devices linked to your network
Smart homes increase comfort—but also increase points of control and surveillance.
In India, lack of awareness + shared living situations make misuse more common.
Control over devices = control over privacy

blocks.blockLabel: Mobile phones are the most personal and data-rich devices we use daily. In India, smartphones are used for
items: Popular devices include brands like Samsung, Xiaomi, and Apple.
Because of this, phones become the primary target for surveillance, stalking, and digital control.
UPI apps like Google Pay, PhonePe, Paytm
This makes phones a complete digital profile of a person’s life.

blocks.blockLabel: Software secretly installed on a phone to
Many apps available online in India claim
items: These are often illegal surveillance tools
Access to OTP (via SIM or phone)
SIM cards registered in someone else’s name
Know where you are at any moment
Once granted, apps can collect sensitive data silently.

subSections.subTitle: 6. WhatsApp & Social Media Risks (Very Important in India)

items: WhatsApp → Linked devices → log out all
Avoid APK downloads from unknown websites

blocks.blockLabel: Whoever controls your phone or its accounts can

items: In India, mobile phones are the central point of digital life.

blocks.blockLabel: Computers (laptops/desktops) and online accounts act as the central hub of digital identity. In India, they are widely used for
items: Devices from companies like HP, Dell, and Lenovo are commonly used.
Unlike phones, computers often store long-term, high-value data, making them a major target for misuse.

sections.sectionTitle: What Data Do Computers & Accounts Store?

items: If email is compromised → entire digital identity is at risk
Trick users into entering login details

blocks.blockLabel: Anyone with access to the computer can

subSections.subTitle: 5. Public & Shared Computer Risks (Very Important in India)

items: Logging into email in a cyber café → forgetting to log out
Next user can access your account
Keep data safe in case of attack
Use unique passwords for each account
Avoid saving passwords on shared systems
Computers and online accounts are the gateway to your entire digital life.
In India, shared usage + phishing scams make them highly vulnerable.
Protecting your accounts = protecting your identity

blocks.blockLabel: In India, these technologies are becoming more common due to
items: While designed for safety and convenience, these tools can be misused for stalking, surveillance, and control.
Tracking technologies are tools used to monitor a person’s location, movement, and behaviour in real time or over a period.

subSections.subTitle: 1. GPS Tracking Devices

items: Use GPS satellites to determine location
Send data via SIM card or internet to a mobile app
But can be misused for stalking
Small electronic devices that use satellite signals to track location.
Connect to nearby smartphones via Bluetooth
Use crowdsourced networks to update location

blocks.blockLabel: Even though some alerts exist, they may

items: Repeated sightings of the same person
Being asked to constantly share location
Turn OFF live location when not needed
Tracking technologies can silently remove your freedom of movement and privacy.
In India, easy availability + low awareness makes misuse more common.
Awareness and regular checking = main protection
Accounts often shared among family members
Daily routine patterns (wake time, activity time)
Sync with mobile apps and cloud accounts
If account is accessed → full activity data visible
Can be misused if leaked or accessed
Increasing use among youth and fitness users
Often synced with shared family devices/accounts
Payment made by tapping card on machine
Small payments can be made without PIN (within limit)
Card Skimming / Cloning: Data can be captured using unauthorized devices
Loss or Theft Misuse: Anyone can tap and pay until limit is reached
Linked Financial Exposure: Connected to bank accounts → financial risk
One account compromised = entire digital life exposed

blocks.blockLabel: Everyday apps and connected services are deeply integrated into modern life in India. They are used for
items: These systems continuously collect and process personal, behavioural, and location-based data.
While they improve convenience, they also create multiple points of data exposure and surveillance.
Stay logged in on shared devices
Direct access to money via apps/cards
Monitoring through shared or compromised accounts
OTP requests not initiated by you

blocks.blockLabel: In India, risks are amplified due to
items: Managing app access and permissions is essential for privacy, safety, and financial security.
Everyday apps and connected services create a fully integrated digital ecosystem.
